title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for lunch or dinner!
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: Tips for the Best Murgh Makhani
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with basmati rice and naan bread, garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) – a culinary delight!

description: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian classic known for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender marinated chicken. This iconic dish offers a perfect balance of sweet, savory, and subtly spicy flavors, making it a comforting and satisfying meal. Enjoy the authentic taste of India with this homemade recipe, perfect for any special occasion or a delicious weeknight treat.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and a pinch of salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary, and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 1 tablespoon of butter. Add green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Saute for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Gravy Ingredients
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes and soaked cashews (drained). Add 1/2 cup of water, sugar, and salt to taste. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.

instructions.title: Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Carefully remove the whole spices (cardamom, cinnamon, bay leaf) if you prefer a smoother texture. Transfer the gravy mixture to a blender or use an immersion blender. Blend until very smooth and creamy. If needed, add a splash of water to achieve desired consistency.

instructions.title: Finish the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Pour the blended gravy back into the pan. Stir in the cooked chicken. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter and the heavy cream. If using, add crushed fenugreek leaves. Simmer gently for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is heated through and slightly thickened. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve your authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti. Enjoy this rich and flavorful Indian classic!

tips: For a truly authentic taste, do not skip the fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi); they add a distinct aroma and flavor to the butter chicken.
Soaking cashews before blending is crucial for achieving a super smooth and creamy gravy texture. You can substitute cashews with blanched almonds for a similar effect.
Always cook the chicken in batches to ensure it browns properly and does not steam, which can make it tough. Adjust the sugar and chili powder to your preferred level of sweetness and spice.
article:
Discover the culinary magic of Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani, a dish that has captivated palates worldwide with its rich, creamy, and subtly spiced profile. This beloved Indian lunch recipe offers a symphony of flavors, making it a perennial favorite for anyone seeking comfort and indulgence in their midday meal. Originating from the bustling kitchens of Delhi, Butter Chicken has evolved from a simple tandoori chicken curry into a global phenomenon, celebrated for its unique balance of tangy tomatoes, fragrant spices, and a luxurious finish of butter and cream. Perfect for a special family lunch or a comforting weekday treat, mastering this dish at home promises a delightful culinary adventure.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter," a name that perfectly encapsulates its essence. This iconic dish emerged in the 1950s from the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, a culinary innovation born from leftover tandoori chicken simmered in a rich tomato-based gravy. The beauty of Murgh Makhani lies in its complex yet harmonious flavor profile: a tangy sweetness from tomatoes, a subtle warmth from aromatic spices like cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves, and an unmistakable creaminess from butter and fresh cream. Unlike spicier curries, Butter Chicken offers a milder, more approachable taste, making it incredibly popular among all ages and a perfect introduction to Indian cuisine. It’s more than just a chicken curry; it’s a culinary hug, a dish that nourishes both body and soul, making it an ideal choice for a satisfying Indian lunch.

## Ingredients for Authentic Butter Chicken

Crafting an authentic Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of ingredients, divided into two main components: the chicken marinade and the rich makhani gravy.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   Salt to taste

For the Murgh Makhani Gravy:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter (plus extra for finishing)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   2 green chilies, slit (optional, for mild heat)
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 fresh large tomatoes, pureed
*   1/2 cup cashew nuts, soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1/4 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1 bay leaf
*   2-3 green cardamom pods
*   1 cinnamon stick (1-inch piece)
*   2 cloves
*   1 tablespoon sugar (adjust to taste)
*   1/2 cup fresh cream (heavy cream)
*   1 tablespoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Recipe

Follow these steps to create your own delicious Murgh Makhani:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** You have a few options:
    *   **Pan-fry:** Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in batches and cook until lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Set aside.
    *   **Bake:** Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Arrange chicken on a baking sheet and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until cooked through and slightly charred.
    *   **Grill:** If you have a grill or tandoor, grill the chicken until tender and smoky.
3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the bay leaf, cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and cloves. Sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
4.  **Sauté Aromatics:** Add the chopped onions and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), and cook for another minute until fragrant.
5.  **Add Tomatoes and Spices:** Pour in the crushed tomatoes (or fresh puree). Add red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and cumin powder. Mix well and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down and the oil starts to separate.
6.  **Incorporate Cashews:** Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the tomato mixture. Cook for 2-3 minutes.
7.  **Simmer and Blend:** Add about 1/2 cup of water, bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld and cashews to soften further. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender (remove whole spices like bay leaf, cinnamon stick first if you prefer a smoother texture without them) and blend until completely smooth. This step is crucial for the silky texture of Butter Chicken.
8.  **Strain the Gravy (Optional but Recommended):** For an extra smooth gravy, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the clean pot, pressing down with a spoon to extract all the liquid. Discard any solids.
9.  **Finish the Gravy:** Return the pot to medium-low heat. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces, sugar, and salt to taste. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the gravy’s flavors.
10. **Add Cream and Kasoori Methi:** Reduce heat to low. Stir in the fresh cream and the crushed kasoori methi. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, just until heated through; do not boil after adding cream as it may curdle.
11. **Garnish and Serve:** Drizzle with a little extra butter or a swirl of cream for garnish, and sprinkle with fresh chopped cilantro. Your homemade Butter Chicken is ready!

## Tips for the Best Murgh Makhani

Achieving restaurant-quality Murgh Makhani at home is entirely possible with a few key tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. Longer marination means more tender, flavorful chicken.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Use fresh, ripe tomatoes and good quality butter and cream. Kashmiri red chili powder is recommended for its vibrant color and mild heat.
*   **Smooth Gravy:** Blending the gravy thoroughly until absolutely smooth is paramount. Straining it afterwards takes it to the next level of silkiness.
*   **Kasoori Methi is Essential:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) impart a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic flavor that is characteristic of authentic Butter Chicken. Don't skip it! Rub it between your palms before adding to release its aroma.
*   **Don't Overcook Chicken:** Cook the chicken until just done before adding it to the gravy to prevent it from becoming tough. It will continue to cook slightly in the sauce.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Adjust sugar, salt, and chili to your preference. The balance of sweet, tangy, and savory is what makes this dish so appealing.
*   **Rest Before Serving:** Like many curries, Butter Chicken often tastes even better the next day, as the flavors have more time to meld.

## Serving Suggestions for Butter Chicken

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a truly satisfying Indian lunch.

*   **Breads:** The classic pairing is with warm, fluffy naan bread, perfect for scooping up every last bit of the rich gravy. Other excellent choices include tandoori roti, garlic naan, or laccha paratha.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a light contrast to the richness of the curry and helps soak up the delicious sauce.
*   **Sides:** A simple side salad of sliced cucumbers, onions, and tomatoes, or a cooling raita (yogurt dip) can cut through the richness and add a refreshing element to your meal. A wedge of lemon on the side is also a great addition for a touch of brightness.
*   **Pickles & Chutneys:** A small serving of Indian pickle (achar) or mango chutney can add an extra layer of flavor and spice.

## Variations & Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is a masterpiece, you can adapt it to suit different dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Easily transform this dish into a vegetarian delight by substituting chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas. Paneer Makhani is a very popular alternative.
*   **Healthier Version:** For a lighter take, reduce the amount of butter and cream. You can substitute some of the cream with Greek yogurt (stirred in at the very end off the heat to prevent curdling) or evaporated milk. Use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder and green chilies according to your heat preference. For a truly mild dish, omit the green chilies entirely.
*   **Nut-Free Option:** If you have a nut allergy, you can substitute cashews with sunflower seeds, poppy seeds, or simply omit them. The gravy will be slightly thinner but still delicious. A tablespoon of cornstarch slurry can be added to thicken it if needed.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an authentic smoky flavor reminiscent of tandoor-cooked chicken, you can employ the "dhungar" method. Place a small, heat-proof bowl in the center of your finished curry, put a hot piece of charcoal in it, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over the charcoal, and immediately cover the pot for a few minutes to infuse the smoke.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani stands as a testament to the rich and diverse flavors of Indian cuisine. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook looking to expand your repertoire, this recipe promises a deeply satisfying and unforgettable Indian lunch experience. Enjoy the process of creating this iconic dish, and savor every creamy, flavorful bite!
